title: Lechuguilla Deep: The Survey Loop
strategies: best single triangle: string the highest-value triangular loop over 3 stations (reproduces the evaluator's weak reference, ~0.1)
enclose-everything hull: convex hull of all interior stations -- captures every cluster but pays one large perimeter and cannot skip a far/low-value cluster

cluster-selection local search: group formations into clusters, then search over WHICH subset of clusters to enclose and how tightly to hug each (nearest-station convex hulls at several radii), scoring every candidate loop by the true objective and keeping the best -- trades rope against documentation value and beats the enclose-everything hull

stronger metaheuristics: simulated annealing / tabu over the vertex set, prize-collecting polygon construction with boundary smoothing, or beam search over station insertions/removals with an incremental simplicity+containment check
